paper_title: Chemical compositions, bioactive compounds, and physicochemical properties of different purple sweet potato flours

entities.id: Chemical compositions, bioactive compounds, and physicochemical properties of different purple sweet potato flours
entities.description: The research title and main study focus on the quality of purple sweet potato flours.
The primary field of research for the study.
Corresponding author at International University, VNU-HCM.

entities.id: International University, Viet Nam National University, Ho Chi Minh City
entities.description: The academic institution where the authors are affiliated.
Method used to obtain extracts from purple sweet potato flour using 25% ethanol.
Standardized methods (46-10.01, 30-10.01, 08-01.01) used to analyze protein, lipid, and ash.
Methods used to measure total phenolic, flavonoid, and anthocyanin contents.
Equipment used to measure pasting properties of the flour samples.

entities.id: Total phenolic content (202.2 mg FAE/100 g flour)
entities.description: Highest phenolic content achieved by samples from Phu Long village.

entities.id: Total anthocyanin content (6.8 mg cyanidin-3-glucoside/100 g flour)
entities.description: Highest anthocyanin content achieved by samples from Phu Long village.
Maximum water solubility index recorded in the study.
Maximum swelling index observed in samples from Hoa Tan village.
Range of protein content found in the sweet potato flours.
